claim: Depletion of nitric oxide is responsible for vasospasm.
evidence_sentences: Decreased bioavailability of nitric oxide has been associated with the development of cerebral vasospasm.
All 8 control monkeys developed significant vasospasm of the right middle cerebral artery, which was complicated by stroke and death in 1 animal.

Nitrite infusion prevented development of vasospasm (no animals developed significant vasospasm; mean [SD] reduction in right middle cerebral artery area on day 7 after subarachnoid hemorrhage of 8% [9%] in nitrite-treated monkeys vs 47% [5%] in saline-treated controls; P<.001).
